skill.name: design-taste-frontend-v1
skill.description: Frontend design skill that sets baseline configuration (design variance, motion, density) and enforces premium, non-generic UI patterns. Includes anti-slop rules, typography directives, animation guidance, and a curated creative arsenal for React and Next.js projects.
skill.summary: Enforces high-end frontend design standards with anti-slop directives, typography rules, motion physics, and performance guardrails for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: The skill file is a static Markdown design guideline with no executable code. Static scanner flagged 162 false positives: backticks are Markdown code formatting (npm install commands, library names), 'weak crypto' matches are design terminology, 'system recon' matches are architecture discussion. The single URL is picsum.photos, a legitimate placeholder service. No prompt injection or malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: Markdown backticks misinterpreted as code execution
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 126 backtick instances as Ruby/shell backtick execution. These are all legitimate Markdown inline code references for package names, CLI commands, and configuration values. No actual code execution exists in the file.

low_findings.title: External placeholder image URL
low_findings.description: File references https://picsum.photos/ for placeholder images. Picsum is a legitimate, widely-used placeholder image service. No data exfiltration or credential exposure risk.

content.user_title: Generate premium frontend designs without AI slop
content.value_statement: LLM-generated UIs tend to be generic and cliched, with centered heroes, purple gradients, and Inter fonts. This skill provides engineered directives to produce distinctive, high-end frontend designs with proper motion physics, typography, and layout diversification.
actual_capabilities: Sets baseline design configuration: DESIGN_VARIANCE, MOTION_INTENSITY, VISUAL_DENSITY
Enforces typography rules with specific font recommendations (Geist, Satoshi, Cabinet Grotesk)
Guides layout diversification with anti-center-bias and asymmetric grid patterns
Provides motion physics specifications for Framer Motion and CSS animations
Lists forbidden AI design patterns (emojis, generic names, neon glows, Inter font)
Offers creative arsenal of advanced UI concepts (Bento grids, magnetic buttons, glassmorphism)
limitations: Guideline only; requires Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to apply directives to actual code generation
Focused on React/Next.js and Tailwind CSS ecosystems
No executable scripts or automation; serves as prompt context
v1 is preserved for backward compatibility; v2 is a substantial rewrite

use_cases.title: Premium SaaS dashboard generation
use_cases.description: Frontend developers building B2B SaaS products who need distinctive dashboards that avoid generic card-grid layouts and purple-gradient aesthetics.

use_cases.title: Anti-slop design enforcement
use_cases.description: Design-conscious teams using AI coding assistants who want consistent, high-end visual output across multiple generations.

use_cases.title: Motion and animation guidance
use_cases.description: Developers implementing Framer Motion or GSAP animations who need specifications for spring physics, stagger choreography, and performance optimization.

prompt_templates.title: Basic component generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a pricing section for a SaaS landing page following the design-taste-frontend-v1 directives. Use asymmetric layout, avoid centered hero, implement loading and empty states.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick start: ask for a single component with skill guidelines applied

prompt_templates.title: Full page with motion
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a complete landing page with hero, features, and footer. Set MOTION_INTENSITY to 7, use Bento grid for features, implement staggerChildren reveals, and ensure mobile fallback to single column.
prompt_templates.scenario: Full page generation with motion specifications

prompt_templates.title: Dashboard with Bento layout
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a SaaS analytics dashboard using the Motion-Engine Bento Paradigm. Implement all 5 card archetypes: Intelligent List, Command Input, Live Status, Wide Data Stream, and Contextual UI. Use spring physics and perpetual micro-interactions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced dashboard with full Bento implementation

prompt_templates.title: Design system audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the attached React codebase against design-taste-frontend-v1 rules. Identify violations: generic names, Inter font usage, 3-column card layouts, missing loading states, and h-screen usage. Provide corrected versions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Audit existing code against the skill's anti-slop rules

output_examples.input: Create a hero section for a project management tool.
output: Asymmetric layout with text left-aligned, image right-aligned
Headline uses Cabinet Grotesk at text-5xl tracking-tighter
Background uses subtle gradient from #f9fafb to white
CTA button with magnetic hover effect via Framer Motion useMotionValue
Loading skeleton matches final layout dimensions
Mobile fallback: single column, px-4, py-8

output_examples.input: Generate a user profile card with avatar and stats.
output: No generic 'John Doe' name; uses distinctive placeholder name
Avatar uses picsum.photos with a unique seed parameter
Stats show organic numbers like 47.2% or +1 (312) 847-1928
Card has rounded-[2.5rem] with diffusion shadow
Status dot has breathing pulse animation
Labels placed outside and below the card

best_practices: Check package.json before importing any third-party library
Use min-h-[100dvh] instead of h-screen for full-height sections
Isolate perpetual motion animations in dedicated Client Components with React.memo
Animate only transform and opacity for GPU-accelerated performance
Wrap dynamic lists in AnimatePresence for smooth state transitions
anti_patterns: Using Inter font for premium or creative contexts
Implementing 3-column card layouts for feature sections
Adding emojis to code, markup, text content, or alt text
Using h-screen for hero sections on mobile
Mixing GSAP/ThreeJS with Framer Motion in the same component tree

faq.question: What is the difference between v1 and v2 of this skill?
faq.answer: V1 is the original taste-skill preserved for projects requiring exact backward compatibility. V2 (design-taste-frontend) is a substantial rewrite and is the current default.

faq.question: Does this skill execute code or run commands?
faq.answer: No. This is a static Markdown guideline file. It provides design directives and constraints that Claude, Codex, or Claude Code apply when generating code for you.

faq.question: Which frameworks does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill targets React and Next.js projects with Tailwind CSS. It references Framer Motion, GSAP, and ThreeJS for animations.

faq.question: Can I customize the baseline configuration values?
faq.answer: Yes. The default values are DESIGN_VARIANCE 8, MOTION_INTENSITY 6, VISUAL_DENSITY 4. You can override these in your prompts based on the UI context.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use in production projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The file contains only design guidelines and reference patterns. No code execution, network calls, or data exfiltration occurs.

faq.question: How do I handle the anti-emoji policy?
faq.answer: The skill bans emojis entirely. Use icon libraries like Phosphor Icons or Radix Icons, or create custom SVG primitives instead.
